title: acer laevigatum subsp. cordatum
intro: Acer laevigatum subsp. cordatum, also known as the Manchurian maple, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to East Asia, characterized by its broad, palmate leaves and red flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe digestive issues and relieve pain. The health benefits of Acer laevigatum subsp. cordatum include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function, making it a popular natural remedy for various ailments. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tincture, tea, or infused oil, which can be used to treat conditions such as fever, wounds, and skin irritations.
